was a [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles")–based record company and label best known for [cool jazz](/wiki/Cool_jazz "Cool jazz") or [West coast jazz](/wiki/West_coast_jazz "West coast jazz"). It was founded in 1952 by producer [Richard Bock](/wiki/Richard_Bock_(record_producer) "Richard Bock (record producer)") (1927–1988)[[1]](#cite_note-1) and drummer [Roy Harte](/wiki/Roy_Harte "Roy Harte") (1924–2003).[[2]](#cite_note-2) Harte, in 1954, also co-founded [Nocturne Records](/wiki/Nocturne_Records "Nocturne Records") with jazz bassist [Harry Babasin](/wiki/Harry_Babasin "Harry Babasin") (1921–1988).[[3]](#cite_note-3)

**Pacific Jazz Records** was a [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles")–based record company and label best known for [cool jazz](/wiki/Cool_jazz "Cool jazz") or [West coast jazz](/wiki/West_coast_jazz "West coast jazz"). It was founded in 1952 by producer [Richard Bock](/wiki/Richard_Bock_(record_producer) "Richard Bock (record producer)") (1927–1988)[[1]](#cite_note-1) and drummer [Roy Harte](/wiki/Roy_Harte "Roy Harte") (1924–2003).[[2]](#cite_note-2) Harte, in 1954, also co-founded [Nocturne Records](/wiki/Nocturne_Records "Nocturne Records") with jazz bassist [Harry Babasin](/wiki/Harry_Babasin "Harry Babasin") (1921–1988).[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Some of the musicians who recorded for Pacific Jazz included [Chet Baker](/wiki/Chet_Baker "Chet Baker"), [Paul Desmond](/wiki/Paul_Desmond "Paul Desmond"), [Gerry Mulligan](/wiki/Gerry_Mulligan "Gerry Mulligan"), [Joe Pass](/wiki/Joe_Pass "Joe Pass"), [Gerald Wilson](/wiki/Gerald_Wilson "Gerald Wilson"), the [Jazz Crusaders](/wiki/Jazz_Crusaders "Jazz Crusaders"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Don Ellis](/wiki/Don_Ellis "Don Ellis"), [Clare Fischer](/wiki/Clare_Fischer "Clare Fischer"), [Jim Hall](/wiki/Jim_Hall_(musician) "Jim Hall (musician)"), [Groove Holmes](/wiki/Groove_Holmes "Groove Holmes"), [Les McCann](/wiki/Les_McCann "Les McCann"), [Wes Montgomery](/wiki/Wes_Montgomery "Wes Montgomery"), and [Art Pepper](/wiki/Art_Pepper "Art Pepper").[[5]](#cite_note-New_Grove-5)

In 1957, Pacific Jazz Records changed its name to **World Pacific Records**[[6]](#cite_note-6) to expand into a full-line label, with the Pacific Jazz imprint retained for jazz releases.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In 1958 Richard Bock and World Pacific were instrumental in introducing Indian traditional music to the West via [Ravi Shankar](/wiki/Ravi_Shankar "Ravi Shankar"), who also recorded for World Pacific.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[5]](#cite_note-New_Grove-5)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Bock sold the label to [Liberty Records](/wiki/Liberty_Records "Liberty Records") in 1965, although he remained as an adviser until 1970. Liberty was merged into and discontinued by [United Artists Records](/wiki/United_Artists_Records "United Artists Records") in 1971; UA in turn was bought by [EMI](/wiki/EMI "EMI") in 1979. [Mosaic](/wiki/Mosaic_Records "Mosaic Records") reissued some Pacific Jazz material in the late 1980s, as did [Blue Note](/wiki/Blue_Note_Records "Blue Note Records") when it gained control of the catalog in the 1990s.[[5]](#cite_note-New_Grove-5)

Releases from Pacific Jazz Records are distributed digitally on streaming platforms through [Universal Music Group](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group").
